revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''三千塚古墳群'''（さんぜんづかこふんぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[東松山市]]にある[[古墳群]]。 頂上に雷電山古墳が築造...'
revision.text:
'''三千塚古墳群'''（さんぜんづかこふんぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[東松山市]]にある[[古墳群]]。 頂上に雷電山古墳が築造されている雷電山から放射状に張り出し、谷によって画された尾根状に9の支群に分かれて250基の古墳が分布していた。この丘陵はゴルフ場造成のため大きく地形が変わり、併せて多くの古墳が消滅した。昭和35年に第3支群の弁天塚古墳が、翌年には第5支群の秋葉塚古墳と第8支群の長塚古墳が調査されている。
* 弁天塚古墳 :第3支群2号墳と編号されている古墳で、第3支群の主墳と考えられる。前方部を北西に向けた墳頂40メートルの前方後円墳である。主体部は竪穴式石室が想定されている。墳丘から土師器、円筒埴輪、人物埴輪が出土。6世紀後半の築造と考えられる。 * 秋葉山古墳 :秋葉塚とも。[[雷電山古墳]]に最も接近して築造された、第5支群の主墳と考えられる墳長44.5メートルの前方後円墳で、1号古墳と編号されている。後円部に全長7メートルの片袖型横穴式石室、前方部に全長1.7メートルの竪穴式石室（石槨）が構築されている。横穴式石室からは鐔2が出土した。埴輪が見つかっていないことから、6世紀末の築造と考えられる。 *長塚古墳 :自然地形を利用して築造された前方後円墳で、1号古墳と編号されている。墳丘長35メートル、後円部径24メートル、前方部長11メートル、前方部幅11メートル。後円部に全長5.7メートルの右片袖型横穴式石室、前方部に全長2.3メートルと推定される竪穴式石室が構築されている。どちらも盗掘を受け、副葬品は横穴式石室から銀環2が出土したのみである。築造時期は6世紀後半、秋葉塚より若干古いと考えられる。
== 参考文献 == 塩野博 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 さきたま出版会、2004年
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[埼玉県の古墳一覧]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さんせんつかこふんくん}} [[category:埼玉県の古墳]]

'''三千塚古墳群'''（さんぜんづかこふんぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[東松山市]]にある[[古墳群]]。 雷電山山頂の雷電山古墳を中心に、放射状に張り出す谷によって画された尾根状に9の支群に分かれて250基の古墳が分布していた。この丘陵はゴルフ場造成のため大きく地形が変わり、併せて多くの古墳が消滅した。昭和35年に第3支群の弁天塚古墳が、翌年には第5支群の秋葉塚古墳と第8支群の長塚古墳が調査されている。 *[[雷電山古墳]] :三千塚古墳群の盟主墳とされる全長85メートルの帆立貝型古墳。墳丘から埼玉県最古の埴輪が出土した。 * 弁天塚古墳 :第3支群2号墳と編号されている古墳で、第3支群の主墳と考えられる。前方部を北西に向けた墳頂40メートルの前方後円墳である。主体部は竪穴式石室が想定されている。墳丘から土師器、円筒埴輪、人物埴輪が出土。6世紀後半の築造と考えられる。 * 秋葉山古墳 :秋葉塚とも。雷電山古墳に最も接近して築造された、第5支群の主墳と考えられる墳長44.5メートルの前方後円墳で、1号古墳と編号されている。後円部に全長7メートルの片袖型横穴式石室、前方部に全長1.7メートルの竪穴式石室（石槨）が構築されている。横穴式石室からは鐔2が出土した。埴輪が見つかっていないことから、6世紀末の築造と考えられる。 *長塚古墳 :自然地形を利用して築造された前方後円墳で、1号古墳と編号されている。墳丘長35メートル、後円部径24メートル、前方部長11メートル、前方部幅11メートル。後円部に全長5.7メートルの右片袖型横穴式石室、前方部に全長2.3メートルと推定される竪穴式石室が構築されている。どちらも盗掘を受け、副葬品は横穴式石室から銀環2が出土したのみである。築造時期は6世紀後半、秋葉塚より若干古いと考えられる。
== 参考文献 == 塩野博 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 さきたま出版会、2004年
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[埼玉県の古墳一覧]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さんせんつかこふんくん}} [[category:埼玉県の古墳]]

'''三千塚古墳群'''（さんぜんづかこふんぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[東松山市]]にある[[古墳群]]。
雷電山山頂の雷電山古墳を中心に、放射状に張り出す谷によって画された尾根状に9の支群に分かれて250基の古墳が分布していた。この丘陵はゴルフ場造成のため大きく地形が変わり、併せて多くの古墳が消滅した。1960年(昭和35年)に第3支群の弁天塚古墳が、翌年には第5支群の秋葉塚古墳と第8支群の長塚古墳が調査されている。
*[[雷電山古墳]] *: 三千塚古墳群の盟主墳とされる全長85メートルの[[帆立貝型古墳]]。墳丘から埼玉県最古の[[埴輪]]が出土した。 * 弁天塚古墳 *: 第3支群2号墳と編号されている古墳で、第3支群の主墳と考えられる。前方部を北西に向けた墳頂40メートルの[[前方後円墳]]である。主体部は[[竪穴式石室]]が想定されている。墳丘から[[土師器]]、[[円筒埴輪]]、人物埴輪が出土。6世紀後半の築造と考えられる。 * 秋葉山古墳 *: 秋葉塚とも。雷電山古墳に最も接近して築造された、第5支群の主墳と考えられる墳長44.5メートルの前方後円墳で、1号古墳と編号されている。後円部に全長7メートルの片袖型[[横穴式石室]]、前方部に全長1.7メートルの竪穴式石室（石槨）が構築されている。横穴式石室からは[[鐔]]2が出土した。埴輪が見つかっていないことから、6世紀末の築造と考えられる。 *長塚古墳 *: 自然地形を利用して築造された前方後円墳で、1号古墳と編号されている。墳丘長35メートル、後円部径24メートル、前方部長11メートル、前方部幅11メートル。後円部に全長5.7メートルの右片袖型横穴式石室、前方部に全長2.3メートルと推定される竪穴式石室が構築されている。どちらも盗掘を受け、副葬品は横穴式石室から銀環2が出土したのみである。築造時期は6世紀後半、秋葉塚より若干古いと考えられる。
== 参考文献 == * 塩野博 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 さきたま出版会、2004年

{{単一の出典|date=2013年10月21日 (月) 07:30 (UTC)}} '''三千塚古墳群'''（さんぜんづかこふんぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[東松山市]]にある[[古墳群]]。
雷電山山頂の雷電山古墳を中心に、放射状に張り出す谷によって画された尾根状に9の支群に分かれて250基の古墳が分布していた。この丘陵はゴルフ場造成のため大きく地形が変わり、併せて多くの古墳が消滅した。1960年(昭和35年)に第3支群の弁天塚古墳が、翌年には第5支群の秋葉塚古墳と第8支群の長塚古墳が調査されている。

{{単一の出典|date=2013年10月21日 (月) 07:30 (UTC)}} '''三千塚古墳群'''（さんぜんづかこふんぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[東松山市]]にある[[古墳群]]。
雷電山山頂の雷電山古墳を中心に、放射状に張り出す谷によって画された尾根状に9の支群に分かれて250基の古墳が分布していた。この丘陵はゴルフ場造成のため大きく地形が変わり、併せて多くの古墳が消滅した。1960年(昭和35年)に第3支群の弁天塚古墳が、翌年には第5支群の秋葉塚古墳と第8支群の長塚古墳が調査されている。
昭和31年2月6日付けで東松山市の史跡に指定された。
== 主な古墳 == *[[雷電山古墳]] *: 三千塚古墳群の盟主墳とされる全長85メートルの[[帆立貝形古墳]]。墳丘から埼玉県最古の[[埴輪]]が出土した。 * 弁天塚古墳 *: 第3支群2号墳と編号されている古墳で、第3支群の主墳と考えられる。前方部を北西に向けた墳頂40メートルの[[前方後円墳]]である。主体部は[[竪穴式石室]]が想定されている。墳丘から[[土師器]]、[[円筒埴輪]]、人物埴輪が出土。6世紀後半の築造と考えられる。 * 秋葉山古墳 *: 秋葉塚とも。雷電山古墳に最も接近して築造された、第5支群の主墳と考えられる墳長44.5メートルの前方後円墳で、1号古墳と編号されている。後円部に全長7メートルの片袖型[[横穴式石室]]、前方部に全長1.7メートルの竪穴式石室（石槨）が構築されている。横穴式石室からは[[鐔]]2が出土した。埴輪が見つかっていないことから、6世紀末の築造と考えられる。 *長塚古墳 *: 自然地形を利用して築造された前方後円墳で、1号古墳と編号されている。墳丘長35メートル、後円部径24メートル、前方部長11メートル、前方部幅11メートル。後円部に全長5.7メートルの右片袖型横穴式石室、前方部に全長2.3メートルと推定される竪穴式石室が構築されている。どちらも盗掘を受け、副葬品は横穴式石室から銀環2が出土したのみである。築造時期は6世紀後半、秋葉塚より若干古いと考えられる。
== 参考文献 == * 塩野博 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 さきたま出版会、2004年
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[埼玉県の古墳一覧]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html 文化財一覧] - 東松山市
{{DEFAULTSORT:さんせんつかこふんくん}} [[category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[Category:東松山市の歴史]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP-11|display=title}} {{Location map |Japan Saitama | lat_deg = 36 | lat_min = 05 | lat_sec = 13.8 | lon_deg = 139 | lon_min = 23 | lon_sec = 17.8 | label = 三千塚
古墳群 | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 200 | caption = {{Center|所在地点}} }} '''三千塚古墳群'''（さんぜんづかこふんぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[東松山市]]大谷にある[[古墳群]]。1956年（昭和31年）2月6日に東松山市の[[史跡]]に指定され{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html|title=文化財一覧|work=東松山市|accessdate=2020-04-04|date=2019-08-22}}、1976年（昭和51年）10月1日には埼玉県選定重要遺跡に選定されている{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/documents/02_116_123.pdf|title=県選定重要遺跡|work=埼玉県|date=2020-03-02|accessdate=2020-04-04}}。 ==概要== 雷電山山頂の[[雷電山古墳 (東松山市)|雷電山古墳]]を中心に、放射状に張り出す谷によって画された尾根状に9の支群に分かれて250基の古墳が分布していた。この丘陵はゴルフ場造成のため大きく地形が変わり、併せて多くの古墳が消滅した。1960年（昭和35年）に第3支群の弁天塚古墳が、翌年には第5支群の秋葉塚古墳と第8支群の長塚古墳が調査されている{{Sfn|東松山市教育委員会|2012}}。 == 主な古墳 == *雷電山古墳 *: 三千塚古墳群の盟主墳とされる全長85メートルの[[帆立貝形古墳]]。墳丘から埼玉県最古の[[埴輪]]が出土した。 * 弁天塚古墳 *: 第3支群2号墳と編号されている古墳で、第3支群の主墳と考えられる。前方部を北西に向けた墳頂40メートルの[[前方後円墳]]である。主体部は[[竪穴式石室]]が想定されている。墳丘から[[土師器]]、[[円筒埴輪]]、人物埴輪が出土。6世紀後半の築造と考えられる。 * 秋葉山古墳 *: 秋葉塚とも。雷電山古墳に最も接近して築造された、第5支群の主墳と考えられる墳長44.5メートルの前方後円墳で、1号古墳と編号されている。後円部に全長7メートルの片袖型[[横穴式石室]]、前方部に全長1.7メートルの竪穴式石室（石槨）が構築されている。横穴式石室からは[[鐔]]2が出土した。埴輪が見つかっていないことから、6世紀末の築造と考えられる。 *長塚古墳 *: 自然地形を利用して築造された前方後円墳で、1号古墳と編号されている。墳丘長35メートル、後円部径24メートル、前方部長11メートル、前方部幅11メートル。後円部に全長5.7メートルの右片袖型横穴式石室、前方部に全長2.3メートルと推定される竪穴式石室が構築されている。どちらも盗掘を受け、副葬品は横穴式石室から銀環2が出土したのみである。築造時期は6世紀後半、秋葉塚より若干古いと考えられる。 ==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *{{Cite book|和書|last=塩野|first=博|title=埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父|publisher=さきたま出版会|year=2004|date=2004-09|ncid=BA68966639|isbn=9784878913822|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|和書|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/50191|author=東松山市教育委員会|title=東松山市発掘調査報告書29:三千塚古墳群|publisher=東松山市|year=2012|date=2012-03-31|ncid=BB12191847|ref=harv}} == 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[埼玉県の古墳一覧]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html 文化財一覧] - 東松山市 {{考古学}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さんせんつかこふんくん}} [[category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[Category:東松山市の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP-11|display=title}} {{Location map |Japan Saitama | lat_deg = 36 | lat_min = 05 | lat_sec = 13.8 | lon_deg = 139 | lon_min = 23 | lon_sec = 17.8 | label = 三千塚
古墳群 | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 200 | caption = {{Center|所在地点}} }} '''三千塚古墳群'''（さんぜんづかこふんぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[東松山市]]大谷にある[[古墳群]]。1956年（昭和31年）2月6日に東松山市の[[史跡]]に指定され{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html|title=文化財一覧|work=東松山市|accessdate=2020-04-04|date=2019-08-22}}、1976年（昭和51年）10月1日には埼玉県選定重要遺跡に選定されている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/documents/02_116_123.pdf|title=県選定重要遺跡|work=埼玉県|date=2020-03-02|accessdate=2020-04-04}}。 ==概要== 雷電山山頂の[[雷電山古墳 (東松山市)|雷電山古墳]]を中心に、放射状に張り出す谷によって画された尾根状に9の支群に分かれて250基の古墳が分布していた。この丘陵はゴルフ場造成のため大きく地形が変わり、併せて多くの古墳が消滅した。1960年（昭和35年）に第3支群の弁天塚古墳が、翌年には第5支群の秋葉塚古墳と第8支群の長塚古墳が調査されている{{Sfn|東松山市教育委員会|2012}}。 == 主な古墳 == *雷電山古墳 *: 三千塚古墳群の盟主墳とされる全長85メートルの[[帆立貝形古墳]]。墳丘から埼玉県最古の[[埴輪]]が出土した。 * 弁天塚古墳 *: 第3支群2号墳と編号されている古墳で、第3支群の主墳と考えられる。前方部を北西に向けた墳頂40メートルの[[前方後円墳]]である。主体部は[[竪穴式石室]]が想定されている。墳丘から[[土師器]]、[[円筒埴輪]]、人物埴輪が出土。6世紀後半の築造と考えられる。 * 秋葉山古墳 *: 秋葉塚とも。雷電山古墳に最も接近して築造された、第5支群の主墳と考えられる墳長44.5メートルの前方後円墳で、1号古墳と編号されている。後円部に全長7メートルの片袖型[[横穴式石室]]、前方部に全長1.7メートルの竪穴式石室（石槨）が構築されている。横穴式石室からは[[鐔]]2が出土した。埴輪が見つかっていないことから、6世紀末の築造と考えられる。 *長塚古墳 *: 自然地形を利用して築造された前方後円墳で、1号古墳と編号されている。墳丘長35メートル、後円部径24メートル、前方部長11メートル、前方部幅11メートル。後円部に全長5.7メートルの右片袖型横穴式石室、前方部に全長2.3メートルと推定される竪穴式石室が構築されている。どちらも盗掘を受け、副葬品は横穴式石室から銀環2が出土したのみである。築造時期は6世紀後半、秋葉塚より若干古いと考えられる。 ファイル:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀
{{small|[[埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館]]展示。}} ファイル:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪
{{small|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館展示。}} ==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *{{Cite book|和書|last=塩野|first=博|title=埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父|publisher=さきたま出版会|year=2004|date=2004-09|ncid=BA68966639|isbn=9784878913822|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|和書|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/50191|author=東松山市教育委員会|title=東松山市発掘調査報告書29:三千塚古墳群|publisher=東松山市|year=2012|date=2012-03-31|ncid=BB12191847|ref=harv}} == 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[埼玉県の古墳一覧]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html 文化財一覧] - 東松山市 {{考古学}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さんせんつかこふんくん}} [[category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[Category:東松山市の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

Kofun Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 200 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster''' (三千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松..."
revision.text:
{{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP-11|display=title}} {{Location map |Japan Saitama | lat_deg = 36 | lat_min = 05 | lat_sec = 13.8 | lon_deg = 139 | lon_min = 23 | lon_sec = 17.8 | label = Sanzenzuka
Kofun Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 200 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster''' (三千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It was designated a Historic Site by Higashimatsuyama City on February 6, 1956,{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2019-08-22|accessdate=2020-04-04}} and selected as an Important Site by Saitama Prefecture on October 1, 1976.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/documents/02_116_123.pdf|title=Important Sites Selected by Saitama Prefecture|publisher=Saitama Prefecture|date=2020-03-02|accessdate=2020-04-04}} == Overview == Centered around the summit of {{ill|Raidenyama Kofun (Higashimatsuyama)|ja|雷電山古墳 (東松山市)}}, the Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster consists of approximately 250 tumuli distributed across nine sub-clusters on ridges divided by valleys extending radially from the summit. The landscape was significantly altered by the development of a golf course, leading to the destruction of many tumuli. The Benten-zuka Kofun in the third sub-cluster was excavated in 1960, followed by the Akiba-zuka Kofun in the fifth sub-cluster and the Nagatsuka Kofun in the eighth sub-cluster in the following year.{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == Major Kofun == * '''Raidenyama Kofun''' : Considered the main tumulus of the cluster, it is an {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|en|帆立貝形古墳}} with a total length of 85 meters. The oldest {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} in Saitama Prefecture was unearthed from this mound. * '''Benten-zuka Kofun''' : Numbered as the second mound of the third sub-cluster and considered its main tumulus. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} with a total length of 40 meters, oriented northwest. The burial chamber is presumed to be a vertical stone chamber. Artifacts such as {{ill|haji ware|en|土師器}}, cylindrical haniwa, and human-shaped haniwa were found, and it is thought to have been built in the late 6th century. * '''Akibayama Kofun''' : Also called Akiba-zuka. It is the main tumulus of the fifth sub-cluster and is closest to Raidenyama Kofun. The tumulus is a 44.5-meter-long keyhole-shaped mound, with a 7-meter-long lateral stone chamber in the rear circular section and a 1.7-meter-long vertical stone chamber in the front section. Two tsuba (sword guards) were excavated from the lateral stone chamber. The absence of haniwa suggests it was built in the late 6th century. * '''Nagatsuka Kofun''' : A keyhole-shaped tumulus utilizing natural topography, numbered as the first mound of the eighth sub-cluster. The tumulus is 35 meters in length, with a rear circular section diameter of 24 meters and a front section length and width of 11 meters. The rear section contains a 5.7-meter-long right-sided lateral stone chamber, and the front section presumably contains a 2.3-meter-long vertical stone chamber. Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}} File:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated horse-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|last=Shiono|first=Hiroshi|title=Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|year=2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == See Also == *{{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} *{{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} == External Links == *[http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City {{Archaeology}} [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Higashimatsuyama History]] [[Category:Kofun Clusters]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[ja:三千塚古墳群]]

It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} with a total length of 40 meters, oriented northwest. The burial chamber is presumed to be a vertical stone chamber. Artifacts such as {{ill|haji ware|en|土師器}}, cylindrical haniwa, and human-shaped haniwa were found, and it is thought to have been built in the late 6th century. * '''Akibayama Kofun''' : Also called Akiba-zuka. It is the main tumulus of the fifth sub-cluster and is closest to Raidenyama Kofun. The tumulus is a 44.5-meter-long keyhole-shaped mound, with a 7-meter-long lateral stone chamber in the rear circular section and a 1.7-meter-long vertical stone chamber in the front section. The tumulus is 35 meters in length, with a rear circular section diameter of 24 meters and a front section length and width of 11 meters. The rear section contains a 5.7-meter-long right-sided lateral stone chamber, and the front section presumably contains a 2.3-meter-long vertical stone chamber. Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}} File:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated horse-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|last=Shiono|first=Hiroshi|title=Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|year=2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == See Also == *{{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} *{{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} == External Links == *[http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City {{Archaeology}} [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Higashimatsuyama History]] [[Category:Kofun Clusters]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[ja:三千塚古墳群]]

revision.text:
{{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP-11|display=title}} {{Location map |Japan Saitama | lat_deg = 36 | lat_min = 05 | lat_sec = 13.8 | lon_deg = 139 | lon_min = 23 | lon_sec = 17.8 | label = Sanzenzuka
Kofun Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 200 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster''' (三千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It was designated a Historic Site by Higashimatsuyama City on February 6, 1956,{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2019-08-22|accessdate=2020-04-04}} and selected as an Important Site by Saitama Prefecture on October 1, 1976.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/documents/02_116_123.pdf|title=Important Sites Selected by Saitama Prefecture|publisher=Saitama Prefecture|date=2020-03-02|accessdate=2020-04-04}} == Overview == Centered around the summit of {{ill|Raidenyama Kofun (Higashimatsuyama)|ja|雷電山古墳 (東松山市)}}, the Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster consists of approximately 250 tumuli distributed across nine sub-clusters on ridges divided by valleys extending radially from the summit. The landscape was significantly altered by the development of a golf course, leading to the destruction of many tumuli. The oldest {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} in Saitama Prefecture was unearthed from this mound. * '''Benten-zuka Kofun''' : Numbered as the second mound of the third sub-cluster and considered its main tumulus. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} with a total length of 40 meters, oriented northwest. The burial chamber is presumed to be a vertical stone chamber. {{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP-11|display=title}} {{Location map |Japan Saitama | lat_deg = 36 | lat_min = 05 | lat_sec = 13.8 | lon_deg = 139 | lon_min = 23 | lon_sec = 17.8 | label = Sanzenzuka
Kofun Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 200 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster''' (三千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It was designated a Historic Site by Higashimatsuyama City on February 6, 1956,{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2019-08-22|accessdate=2020-04-04}} and selected as an Important Site by Saitama Prefecture on October 1, 1976.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/documents/02_116_123.pdf|title=Important Sites Selected by Saitama Prefecture|publisher=Saitama Prefecture|date=2020-03-02|accessdate=2020-04-04}} == Overview == Centered around the summit of {{ill|Raidenyama Kofun (Higashimatsuyama)|ja|雷電山古墳 (東松山市)}}, the Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster consists of approximately 250 tumuli distributed across nine sub-clusters on ridges divided by valleys extending radially from the summit. The landscape was significantly altered by the development of a golf course, leading to the destruction of many tumuli. The Benten-zuka Kofun in the third sub-cluster was excavated in 1960, followed by the Akiba-zuka Kofun in the fifth sub-cluster and the Nagatsuka Kofun in the eighth sub-cluster in the following year.{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == Major Kofun == * '''Raidenyama Kofun''' : Considered the main tumulus of the cluster, it is an {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|en|帆立貝形古墳}} with a total length of 85 meters. The oldest {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} in Saitama Prefecture was unearthed from this mound. * '''Benten-zuka Kofun''' : Numbered as the second mound of the third sub-cluster and considered its main tumulus. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} with a total length of 40 meters, oriented northwest. The burial chamber is presumed to be a vertical stone chamber. Artifacts such as {{ill|haji ware|en|土師器}}, cylindrical haniwa, and human-shaped haniwa were found, and it is thought to have been built in the late 6th century. * '''Akibayama Kofun''' : Also called Akiba-zuka. It is the main tumulus of the fifth sub-cluster and is closest to Raidenyama Kofun. The tumulus is a 44.5-meter-long keyhole-shaped mound, with a 7-meter-long lateral stone chamber in the rear circular section and a 1.7-meter-long vertical stone chamber in the front section. Two tsuba (sword guards) were excavated from the lateral stone chamber. The absence of haniwa suggests it was built in the late 6th century. * '''Nagatsuka Kofun''' : A keyhole-shaped tumulus utilizing natural topography, numbered as the first mound of the eighth sub-cluster. The tumulus is 35 meters in length, with a rear circular section diameter of 24 meters and a front section length and width of 11 meters. The rear section contains a 5.7-meter-long right-sided lateral stone chamber, and the front section presumably contains a 2.3-meter-long vertical stone chamber. Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}} File:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated horse-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|last=Shiono|first=Hiroshi|title=Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|year=2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == See Also == *{{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} *{{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} == External Links == *[http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City {{Archaeology}} [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Higashimatsuyama History]] [[Category:Kofun Clusters]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:01-17]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[ja:三千塚古墳群]]

The Benten-zuka Kofun in the third sub-cluster was excavated in 1960, followed by the Akiba-zuka Kofun in the fifth sub-cluster and the Nagatsuka Kofun in the eighth sub-cluster in the following year.{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == Major Kofun == * '''Raidenyama Kofun''' : Considered the main tumulus of the cluster, it is an {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|en|帆立貝形古墳}} with a total length of 85 meters. The oldest {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} in Saitama Prefecture was unearthed from this mound. * '''Benten-zuka Kofun''' : Numbered as the second mound of the third sub-cluster and considered its main tumulus. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} with a total length of 40 meters, oriented northwest. The burial chamber is presumed to be a vertical stone chamber. Artifacts such as {{ill|haji ware|en|土師器}}, cylindrical haniwa, and human-shaped haniwa were found, and it is thought to have been built in the late 6th century. * '''Akibayama Kofun''' : Also called Akiba-zuka. It is the main tumulus of the fifth sub-cluster and is closest to Raidenyama Kofun. The tumulus is a 44.5-meter-long keyhole-shaped mound, with a 7-meter-long lateral stone chamber in the rear circular section and a 1.7-meter-long vertical stone chamber in the front section. Two tsuba (sword guards) were excavated from the lateral stone chamber. The absence of haniwa suggests it was built in the late 6th century. * '''Nagatsuka Kofun''' : A keyhole-shaped tumulus utilizing natural topography, numbered as the first mound of the eighth sub-cluster. The tumulus is 35 meters in length, with a rear circular section diameter of 24 meters and a front section length and width of 11 meters. The rear section contains a 5.7-meter-long right-sided lateral stone chamber, and the front section presumably contains a 2.3-meter-long vertical stone chamber. Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}} File:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated horse-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|last=Shiono|first=Hiroshi|title=Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|year=2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == See Also == *{{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} *{{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} == External Links == *[http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City {{Archaeology}} [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Higashimatsuyama History]] [[Category:Kofun Clusters]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:01-17]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:三千塚古墳群]]

Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. Kofun Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 200 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster''' (三千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It was designated a Historic Site by Higashimatsuyama City on February 6, 1956,{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2019-08-22|accessdate=2020-04-04}} and selected as an Important Site by Saitama Prefecture on October 1, 1976.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/documents/02_116_123.pdf|title=Important Sites Selected by Saitama Prefecture|publisher=Saitama Prefecture|date=2020-03-02|accessdate=2020-04-04}} == Overview == Centered around the summit of {{ill|Raidenyama Kofun (Higashimatsuyama)|ja|雷電山古墳 (東松山市)}}, the Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster consists of approximately 250 tumuli distributed across nine sub-clusters on ridges divided by valleys extending radially from the summit. The landscape was significantly altered by the development of a golf course, leading to the destruction of many tumuli. The Benten-zuka Kofun in the third sub-cluster was excavated in 1960, followed by the Akiba-zuka Kofun in the fifth sub-cluster and the Nagatsuka Kofun in the eighth sub-cluster in the following year.{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == Major Kofun == * '''Raidenyama Kofun''' : Considered the main tumulus of the cluster, it is an {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|en|帆立貝形古墳}} with a total length of 85 meters. The oldest {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} in Saitama Prefecture was unearthed from this mound. * '''Benten-zuka Kofun''' : Numbered as the second mound of the third sub-cluster and considered its main tumulus. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} with a total length of 40 meters, oriented northwest. The burial chamber is presumed to be a vertical stone chamber. Artifacts such as {{ill|haji ware|en|土師器}}, cylindrical haniwa, and human-shaped haniwa were found, and it is thought to have been built in the late 6th century. * '''Akibayama Kofun''' : Also called Akiba-zuka. It is the main tumulus of the fifth sub-cluster and is closest to Raidenyama Kofun. The tumulus is a 44.5-meter-long keyhole-shaped mound, with a 7-meter-long lateral stone chamber in the rear circular section and a 1.7-meter-long vertical stone chamber in the front section. Two tsuba (sword guards) were excavated from the lateral stone chamber. The absence of haniwa suggests it was built in the late 6th century. * '''Nagatsuka Kofun''' : A keyhole-shaped tumulus utilizing natural topography, numbered as the first mound of the eighth sub-cluster. The tumulus is 35 meters in length, with a rear circular section diameter of 24 meters and a front section length and width of 11 meters. The rear section contains a 5.7-meter-long right-sided lateral stone chamber, and the front section presumably contains a 2.3-meter-long vertical stone chamber. Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}} File:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated horse-shaped haniwa

revision.text:
{{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP-11|display=title}} {{Location map |Japan Saitama | lat_deg = 36 | lat_min = 05 | lat_sec = 13.8 | lon_deg = 139 | lon_min = 23 | lon_sec = 17.8 | label = Sanzenzuka
Kofun Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 200 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster''' (三千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It was designated a Historic Site by Higashimatsuyama City on February 6, 1956,{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2019-08-22|accessdate=2020-04-04}} and selected as an Important Site by Saitama Prefecture on October 1, 1976.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/documents/02_116_123.pdf|title=Important Sites Selected by Saitama Prefecture|publisher=Saitama Prefecture|date=2020-03-02|accessdate=2020-04-04}} == Overview == Centered around the summit of {{ill|Raidenyama Kofun (Higashimatsuyama)|ja|雷電山古墳 (東松山市)}}, the Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster consists of approximately 250 tumuli distributed across nine sub-clusters on ridges divided by valleys extending radially from the summit. The landscape was significantly altered by the development of a golf course, leading to the destruction of many tumuli. The Benten-zuka Kofun in the third sub-cluster was excavated in 1960, followed by the Akiba-zuka Kofun in the fifth sub-cluster and the Nagatsuka Kofun in the eighth sub-cluster in the following year.{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == Major Kofun == * '''Raidenyama Kofun''' : Considered the main tumulus of the cluster, it is an {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|en|帆立貝形古墳}} with a total length of 85 meters. The oldest {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} in Saitama Prefecture was unearthed from this mound. * '''Benten-zuka Kofun''' : Numbered as the second mound of the third sub-cluster and considered its main tumulus. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} with a total length of 40 meters, oriented northwest. The burial chamber is presumed to be a vertical stone chamber. Artifacts such as {{ill|haji ware|en|土師器}}, cylindrical haniwa, and human-shaped haniwa were found, and it is thought to have been built in the late 6th century. * '''Akibayama Kofun''' : Also called Akiba-zuka. It is the main tumulus of the fifth sub-cluster and is closest to Raidenyama Kofun. The tumulus is a 44.5-meter-long keyhole-shaped mound, with a 7-meter-long lateral stone chamber in the rear circular section and a 1.7-meter-long vertical stone chamber in the front section. Two tsuba (sword guards) were excavated from the lateral stone chamber. The absence of haniwa suggests it was built in the late 6th century. * '''Nagatsuka Kofun''' : A keyhole-shaped tumulus utilizing natural topography, numbered as the first mound of the eighth sub-cluster. The tumulus is 35 meters in length, with a rear circular section diameter of 24 meters and a front section length and width of 11 meters. The rear section contains a 5.7-meter-long right-sided lateral stone chamber, and the front section presumably contains a 2.3-meter-long vertical stone chamber. Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}} File:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated horse-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|last=Shiono|first=Hiroshi|title=Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|year=2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == See Also == *{{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} *{{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} == External Links == *[http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City {{Archaeology}} [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Higashimatsuyama History]] [[Category:Kofun Clusters]] [[Category:Adding a category]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:01-17]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[ja:三千塚古墳群]]

{{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP-11|display=title}} {{Location map |Japan Saitama | lat_deg = 36 | lat_min = 05 | lat_sec = 13.8 | lon_deg = 139 | lon_min = 23 | lon_sec = 17.8 | label = Sanzenzuka
Kofun Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 200 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster''' (三千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It was designated a Historic Site by Higashimatsuyama City on February 6, 1956,{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2019-08-22|accessdate=2020-04-04}} and selected as an Important Site by Saitama Prefecture on October 1, 1976.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/documents/02_116_123.pdf|title=Important Sites Selected by Saitama Prefecture|publisher=Saitama Prefecture|date=2020-03-02|accessdate=2020-04-04}} == Overview == Centered around the summit of {{ill|Raidenyama Kofun (Higashimatsuyama)|ja|雷電山古墳 (東松山市)}}, the Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster consists of approximately 250 tumuli distributed across nine sub-clusters on ridges divided by valleys extending radially from the summit. The landscape was significantly altered by the development of a golf course, leading to the destruction of many tumuli. The Benten-zuka Kofun in the third sub-cluster was excavated in 1960, followed by the Akiba-zuka Kofun in the fifth sub-cluster and the Nagatsuka Kofun in the eighth sub-cluster in the following year.{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == Major Kofun == * '''Raidenyama Kofun''' : Considered the main tumulus of the cluster, it is an {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|en|帆立貝形古墳}} with a total length of 85 meters. The oldest {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} in Saitama Prefecture was unearthed from this mound. * '''Benten-zuka Kofun''' : Numbered as the second mound of the third sub-cluster and considered its main tumulus. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} with a total length of 40 meters, oriented northwest. The burial chamber is presumed to be a vertical stone chamber. Artifacts such as {{ill|haji ware|en|土師器}}, cylindrical haniwa, and human-shaped haniwa were found, and it is thought to have been built in the late 6th century. * '''Akibayama Kofun''' : Also called Akiba-zuka. It is the main tumulus of the fifth sub-cluster and is closest to Raidenyama Kofun. The tumulus is a 44.5-meter-long keyhole-shaped mound, with a 7-meter-long lateral stone chamber in the rear circular section and a 1.7-meter-long vertical stone chamber in the front section. Two tsuba (sword guards) were excavated from the lateral stone chamber. The absence of haniwa suggests it was built in the late 6th century. * '''Nagatsuka Kofun''' : A keyhole-shaped tumulus utilizing natural topography, numbered as the first mound of the eighth sub-cluster. The tumulus is 35 meters in length, with a rear circular section diameter of 24 meters and a front section length and width of 11 meters. The rear section contains a 5.7-meter-long right-sided lateral stone chamber, and the front section presumably contains a 2.3-meter-long vertical stone chamber. Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}} File:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated horse-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|last=Shiono|first=Hiroshi|title=Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|year=2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == See Also == *{{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} *{{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} == External Links == *[http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City {{Archaeology}} [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Higashimatsuyama History]] [[Category:Kofun Clusters]] [[Category:Adding a category]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:01-17]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:三千塚古墳群]]

revision.text:
{{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP-11|display=title}} {{Location map |Japan Saitama | lat_deg = 36 | lat_min = 05 | lat_sec = 13.8 | lon_deg = 139 | lon_min = 23 | lon_sec = 17.8 | label = Sanzenzuka
Kofun Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 200 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster''' (三千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It was designated a Historic Site by Higashimatsuyama City on February 6, 1956,{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2019-08-22|accessdate=2020-04-04}} and selected as an Important Site by Saitama Prefecture on October 1, 1976.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/documents/02_116_123.pdf|title=Important Sites Selected by Saitama Prefecture|publisher=Saitama Prefecture|date=2020-03-02|accessdate=2020-04-04}} == Overview == Centered around the summit of {{ill|Raidenyama Kofun (Higashimatsuyama)|ja|雷電山古墳 (東松山市)}}, the Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster consists of approximately 250 tumuli distributed across nine sub-clusters on ridges divided by valleys extending radially from the summit. The landscape was significantly altered by the development of a golf course, leading to the destruction of many tumuli. The Benten-zuka Kofun in the third sub-cluster was excavated in 1960, followed by the Akiba-zuka Kofun in the fifth sub-cluster and the Nagatsuka Kofun in the eighth sub-cluster in the following year.{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == Major Kofun == * '''Raidenyama Kofun''' : Considered the main tumulus of the cluster, it is an {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|en|帆立貝形古墳}} with a total length of 85 meters. The oldest {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} in Saitama Prefecture was unearthed from this mound. * '''Benten-zuka Kofun''' : Numbered as the second mound of the third sub-cluster and considered its main tumulus. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} with a total length of 40 meters, oriented northwest. The burial chamber is presumed to be a vertical stone chamber. Artifacts such as {{ill|haji ware|en|土師器}}, cylindrical haniwa, and human-shaped haniwa were found, and it is thought to have been built in the late 6th century. * '''Akibayama Kofun''' : Also called Akiba-zuka. It is the main tumulus of the fifth sub-cluster and is closest to Raidenyama Kofun. The tumulus is a 44.5-meter-long keyhole-shaped mound, with a 7-meter-long lateral stone chamber in the rear circular section and a 1.7-meter-long vertical stone chamber in the front section. Two tsuba (sword guards) were excavated from the lateral stone chamber. The absence of haniwa suggests it was built in the late 6th century. * '''Nagatsuka Kofun''' : A keyhole-shaped tumulus utilizing natural topography, numbered as the first mound of the eighth sub-cluster. The tumulus is 35 meters in length, with a rear circular section diameter of 24 meters and a front section length and width of 11 meters. The rear section contains a 5.7-meter-long right-sided lateral stone chamber, and the front section presumably contains a 2.3-meter-long vertical stone chamber. Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}} File:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated horse-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|last=Shiono|first=Hiroshi|title=Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|year=2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == See Also == *{{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} *{{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} == External Links == *[http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City {{Archaeology}} [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Higashimatsuyama History]] [[Category:Kofun Clusters]] [[Category:Adding a category]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Sanchinzuka Kofun Group-qq]] [[Category:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:01-17]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Sanchinzuka Kofun Group-qq]] [[Category:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] [[ja:三千塚古墳群]]

The tumulus is 35 meters in length, with a rear circular section diameter of 24 meters and a front section length and width of 11 meters. The rear section contains a 5.7-meter-long right-sided lateral stone chamber, and the front section presumably contains a 2.3-meter-long vertical stone chamber. Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)

{{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP-11|display=title}} {{Location map |Japan Saitama | lat_deg = 36 | lat_min = 05 | lat_sec = 13.8 | lon_deg = 139 | lon_min = 23 | lon_sec = 17.8 | label = Sanzenzuka
Kofun Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 200 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster''' (三千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It was designated a Historic Site by Higashimatsuyama City on February 6, 1956,{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2019-08-22|accessdate=2020-04-04}} and selected as an Important Site by Saitama Prefecture on October 1, 1976.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/documents/02_116_123.pdf|title=Important Sites Selected by Saitama Prefecture|publisher=Saitama Prefecture|date=2020-03-02|accessdate=2020-04-04}} == Overview == Centered around the summit of {{ill|Raidenyama Kofun (Higashimatsuyama)|ja|雷電山古墳 (東松山市)}}, the Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster consists of approximately 250 tumuli distributed across nine sub-clusters on ridges divided by valleys extending radially from the summit. The landscape was significantly altered by the development of a golf course, leading to the destruction of many tumuli. The Benten-zuka Kofun in the third sub-cluster was excavated in 1960, followed by the Akiba-zuka Kofun in the fifth sub-cluster and the Nagatsuka Kofun in the eighth sub-cluster in the following year.{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == Major Kofun == * '''Raidenyama Kofun''' : Considered the main tumulus of the cluster, it is an {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|en|帆立貝形古墳}} with a total length of 85 meters. The oldest {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} in Saitama Prefecture was unearthed from this mound. * '''Benten-zuka Kofun''' : Numbered as the second mound of the third sub-cluster and considered its main tumulus. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} with a total length of 40 meters, oriented northwest. The burial chamber is presumed to be a vertical stone chamber. Artifacts such as {{ill|haji ware|en|土師器}}, cylindrical haniwa, and human-shaped haniwa were found, and it is thought to have been built in the late 6th century. * '''Akibayama Kofun''' : Also called Akiba-zuka. It is the main tumulus of the fifth sub-cluster and is closest to Raidenyama Kofun. The tumulus is a 44.5-meter-long keyhole-shaped mound, with a 7-meter-long lateral stone chamber in the rear circular section and a 1.7-meter-long vertical stone chamber in the front section. Two tsuba (sword guards) were excavated from the lateral stone chamber. The absence of haniwa suggests it was built in the late 6th century. * '''Nagatsuka Kofun''' : A keyhole-shaped tumulus utilizing natural topography, numbered as the first mound of the eighth sub-cluster. The tumulus is 35 meters in length, with a rear circular section diameter of 24 meters and a front section length and width of 11 meters. The rear section contains a 5.7-meter-long right-sided lateral stone chamber, and the front section presumably contains a 2.3-meter-long vertical stone chamber. Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}} File:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated horse-shaped haniwa

Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}} File:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated horse-shaped haniwa

It was designated a Historic Site by Higashimatsuyama City on February 6, 1956,{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2019-08-22|accessdate=2020-04-04}} and selected as an Important Site by Saitama Prefecture on October 1, 1976.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/documents/02_116_123.pdf|title=Important Sites Selected by Saitama Prefecture|publisher=Saitama Prefecture|date=2020-03-02|accessdate=2020-04-04}} == Overview == Centered around the summit of {{ill|Raidenyama Kofun (Higashimatsuyama)|ja|雷電山古墳 (東松山市)}}, the Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster consists of approximately 250 tumuli distributed across nine sub-clusters on ridges divided by valleys extending radially from the summit. The landscape was significantly altered by the development of a golf course, leading to the destruction of many tumuli. The Benten-zuka Kofun in the third sub-cluster was excavated in 1960, followed by the Akiba-zuka Kofun in the fifth sub-cluster and the Nagatsuka Kofun in the eighth sub-cluster in the following year.{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == Major Kofun == * '''Raidenyama Kofun''' : Considered the main tumulus of the cluster, it is an {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|en|帆立貝形古墳}} with a total length of 85 meters. The oldest {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} in Saitama Prefecture was unearthed from this mound. * '''Benten-zuka Kofun''' : Numbered as the second mound of the third sub-cluster and considered its main tumulus. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} with a total length of 40 meters, oriented northwest. The burial chamber is presumed to be a vertical stone chamber. Artifacts such as {{ill|haji ware|en|土師器}}, cylindrical haniwa, and human-shaped haniwa were found, and it is thought to have been built in the late 6th century. * '''Akibayama Kofun''' : Also called Akiba-zuka. It is the main tumulus of the fifth sub-cluster and is closest to Raidenyama Kofun. The tumulus is a 44.5-meter-long keyhole-shaped mound, with a 7-meter-long lateral stone chamber in the rear circular section and a 1.7-meter-long vertical stone chamber in the front section. Two tsuba (sword guards) were excavated from the lateral stone chamber. The absence of haniwa suggests it was built in the late 6th century. * '''Nagatsuka Kofun''' : A keyhole-shaped tumulus utilizing natural topography, numbered as the first mound of the eighth sub-cluster. The tumulus is 35 meters in length, with a rear circular section diameter of 24 meters and a front section length and width of 11 meters. The rear section contains a 5.7-meter-long right-sided lateral stone chamber, and the front section presumably contains a 2.3-meter-long vertical stone chamber. Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)

It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} with a total length of 40 meters, oriented northwest. The burial chamber is presumed to be a vertical stone chamber. Artifacts such as {{ill|haji ware|en|土師器}}, cylindrical haniwa, and human-shaped haniwa were found, and it is thought to have been built in the late 6th century. * '''Akibayama Kofun''' : Also called Akiba-zuka. It is the main tumulus of the fifth sub-cluster and is closest to Raidenyama Kofun. The tumulus is a 44.5-meter-long keyhole-shaped mound, with a 7-meter-long lateral stone chamber in the rear circular section and a 1.7-meter-long vertical stone chamber in the front section. Two tsuba (sword guards) were excavated from the lateral stone chamber. The absence of haniwa suggests it was built in the late 6th century. * '''Nagatsuka Kofun''' : A keyhole-shaped tumulus utilizing natural topography, numbered as the first mound of the eighth sub-cluster. The tumulus is 35 meters in length, with a rear circular section diameter of 24 meters and a front section length and width of 11 meters. The rear section contains a 5.7-meter-long right-sided lateral stone chamber, and the front section presumably contains a 2.3-meter-long vertical stone chamber. Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}} File:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated horse-shaped haniwa

The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)

{{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP-11|display=title}} {{Location map |Japan Saitama | lat_deg = 36 | lat_min = 05 | lat_sec = 13.8 | lon_deg = 139 | lon_min = 23 | lon_sec = 17.8 | label = Sanzenzuka
Kofun Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 200 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster''' (三千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It was designated a Historic Site by Higashimatsuyama City on February 6, 1956,{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2019-08-22|accessdate=2020-04-04}} and selected as an Important Site by Saitama Prefecture on October 1, 1976.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/documents/02_116_123.pdf|title=Important Sites Selected by Saitama Prefecture|publisher=Saitama Prefecture|date=2020-03-02|accessdate=2020-04-04}} == Overview == Centered around the summit of {{ill|Raidenyama Kofun (Higashimatsuyama)|ja|雷電山古墳 (東松山市)}}, the Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster consists of approximately 250 tumuli distributed across nine sub-clusters on ridges divided by valleys extending radially from the summit. The landscape was significantly altered by the development of a golf course, leading to the destruction of many tumuli. The Benten-zuka Kofun in the third sub-cluster was excavated in 1960, followed by the Akiba-zuka Kofun in the fifth sub-cluster and the Nagatsuka Kofun in the eighth sub-cluster in the following year.{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == Major Kofun == * '''Raidenyama Kofun''' : Considered the main tumulus of the cluster, it is an {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|en|帆立貝形古墳}} with a total length of 85 meters. The oldest {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} in Saitama Prefecture was unearthed from this mound. * '''Benten-zuka Kofun''' : Numbered as the second mound of the third sub-cluster and considered its main tumulus. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} with a total length of 40 meters, oriented northwest. The burial chamber is presumed to be a vertical stone chamber. Artifacts such as {{ill|haji ware|en|土師器}}, cylindrical haniwa, and human-shaped haniwa were found, and it is thought to have been built in the late 6th century. * '''Akibayama Kofun''' : Also called Akiba-zuka. It is the main tumulus of the fifth sub-cluster and is closest to Raidenyama Kofun. The tumulus is a 44.5-meter-long keyhole-shaped mound, with a 7-meter-long lateral stone chamber in the rear circular section and a 1.7-meter-long vertical stone chamber in the front section. Two tsuba (sword guards) were excavated from the lateral stone chamber. The absence of haniwa suggests it was built in the late 6th century. * '''Nagatsuka Kofun''' : A keyhole-shaped tumulus utilizing natural topography, numbered as the first mound of the eighth sub-cluster. The tumulus is 35 meters in length, with a rear circular section diameter of 24 meters and a front section length and width of 11 meters. The rear section contains a 5.7-meter-long right-sided lateral stone chamber, and the front section presumably contains a 2.3-meter-long vertical stone chamber. Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}} File:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated horse-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|last=Shiono|first=Hiroshi|title=Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|year=2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == See Also == *{{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} *{{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} == External Links == *[http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City {{Archaeology}} [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Higashimatsuyama History]] [[Category:Kofun Clusters]] [[Category:Adding a category]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sanchinzuka Kofun Group-qq]] [[Category:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:01-17]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sanchinzuka Kofun Group-qq]] [[Category:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:三千塚古墳群]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]][[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]][[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:帆立貝形古墳]]

Kofun Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 200 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster''' (三千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. {{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}} File:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated horse-shaped haniwa

The rear section contains a 5.7-meter-long right-sided lateral stone chamber, and the front section presumably contains a 2.3-meter-long vertical stone chamber. Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}} File:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated horse-shaped haniwa

revision.text:
{{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP-11|display=title}} {{Location map |Japan Saitama | lat_deg = 36 | lat_min = 05 | lat_sec = 13.8 | lon_deg = 139 | lon_min = 23 | lon_sec = 17.8 | label = Sanzenzuka
Kofun Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 200 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster''' (三千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It was designated a Historic Site by Higashimatsuyama City on February 6, 1956,{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2019-08-22|accessdate=2020-04-04}} and selected as an Important Site by Saitama Prefecture on October 1, 1976.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/documents/02_116_123.pdf|title=Important Sites Selected by Saitama Prefecture|publisher=Saitama Prefecture|date=2020-03-02|accessdate=2020-04-04}} == Overview == Centered around the summit of {{ill|Raidenyama Kofun (Higashimatsuyama)|ja|雷電山古墳 (東松山市)}}, the Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster consists of approximately 250 tumuli distributed across nine sub-clusters on ridges divided by valleys extending radially from the summit. The landscape was significantly altered by the development of a golf course, leading to the destruction of many tumuli. The Benten-zuka Kofun in the third sub-cluster was excavated in 1960, followed by the Akiba-zuka Kofun in the fifth sub-cluster and the Nagatsuka Kofun in the eighth sub-cluster in the following year.{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == Major Kofun == * '''Raidenyama Kofun''' : Considered the main tumulus of the cluster, it is an {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|en|帆立貝形古墳}} with a total length of 85 meters. The oldest {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} in Saitama Prefecture was unearthed from this mound. * '''Benten-zuka Kofun''' : Numbered as the second mound of the third sub-cluster and considered its main tumulus. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} with a total length of 40 meters, oriented northwest. The burial chamber is presumed to be a vertical stone chamber. Artifacts such as {{ill|haji ware|en|土師器}}, cylindrical haniwa, and human-shaped haniwa were found, and it is thought to have been built in the late 6th century. * '''Akibayama Kofun''' : Also called Akiba-zuka. It is the main tumulus of the fifth sub-cluster and is closest to Raidenyama Kofun. The tumulus is a 44.5-meter-long keyhole-shaped mound, with a 7-meter-long lateral stone chamber in the rear circular section and a 1.7-meter-long vertical stone chamber in the front section. Two tsuba (sword guards) were excavated from the lateral stone chamber. The absence of haniwa suggests it was built in the late 6th century. * '''Nagatsuka Kofun''' : A keyhole-shaped tumulus utilizing natural topography, numbered as the first mound of the eighth sub-cluster. The tumulus is 35 meters in length, with a rear circular section diameter of 24 meters and a front section length and width of 11 meters. The rear section contains a 5.7-meter-long right-sided lateral stone chamber, and the front section presumably contains a 2.3-meter-long vertical stone chamber. Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}} File:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated horse-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|last=Shiono|first=Hiroshi|title=Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|year=2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == See Also == *{{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} *{{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} == External Links == *[http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html List of Cultural Properties] - revision.comment: linking [[Scallop Kofun]]
revision.text:
{{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP-11|display=title}} {{Location map |Japan Saitama | lat_deg = 36 | lat_min = 05 | lat_sec = 13.8 | lon_deg = 139 | lon_min = 23 | lon_sec = 17.8 | label = Sanzenzuka
Kofun Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 200 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster''' (三千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It was designated a Historic Site by Higashimatsuyama City on February 6, 1956,{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2019-08-22|accessdate=2020-04-04}} and selected as an Important Site by Saitama Prefecture on October 1, 1976.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/documents/02_116_123.pdf|title=Important Sites Selected by Saitama Prefecture|publisher=Saitama Prefecture|date=2020-03-02|accessdate=2020-04-04}} == Overview == Centered around the summit of {{ill|Raidenyama Kofun (Higashimatsuyama)|ja|雷電山古墳 (東松山市)}}, the Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster consists of approximately 250 tumuli distributed across nine sub-clusters on ridges divided by valleys extending radially from the summit. The landscape was significantly altered by the development of a golf course, leading to the destruction of many tumuli. The Benten-zuka Kofun in the third sub-cluster was excavated in 1960, followed by the Akiba-zuka Kofun in the fifth sub-cluster and the Nagatsuka Kofun in the eighth sub-cluster in the following year.{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == Major Kofun == * '''Raidenyama Kofun''' : Considered the main tumulus of the cluster, it is an {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|en|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a total length of 85 meters. The oldest {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} in Saitama Prefecture was unearthed from this mound. * '''Benten-zuka Kofun''' : Numbered as the second mound of the third sub-cluster and considered its main tumulus. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} with a total length of 40 meters, oriented northwest. The burial chamber is presumed to be a vertical stone chamber. Artifacts such as {{ill|haji ware|en|土師器}}, cylindrical haniwa, and human-shaped haniwa were found, and it is thought to have been built in the late 6th century. * '''Akibayama Kofun''' : Also called Akiba-zuka. It is the main tumulus of the fifth sub-cluster and is closest to Raidenyama Kofun. The tumulus is a 44.5-meter-long keyhole-shaped mound, with a 7-meter-long lateral stone chamber in the rear circular section and a 1.7-meter-long vertical stone chamber in the front section. Two tsuba (sword guards) were excavated from the lateral stone chamber. The absence of haniwa suggests it was built in the late 6th century. * '''Nagatsuka Kofun''' : A keyhole-shaped tumulus utilizing natural topography, numbered as the first mound of the eighth sub-cluster. The tumulus is 35 meters in length, with a rear circular section diameter of 24 meters and a front section length and width of 11 meters. The rear section contains a 5.7-meter-long right-sided lateral stone chamber, and the front section presumably contains a 2.3-meter-long vertical stone chamber. Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}} File:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated horse-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|last=Shiono|first=Hiroshi|title=Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|year=2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == See Also == *{{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} *{{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} == External Links == *[http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City {{Archaeology}} [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Higashimatsuyama History]] [[Category:Kofun Clusters]] [[Category:Adding a category]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sanchinzuka Kofun Group-qq]] [[Category:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:01-17]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sanchinzuka Kofun Group-qq]] [[Category:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:三千塚古墳群]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]][[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]][[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]][[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]][[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]][[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]][[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]][[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]][[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:帆立貝形古墳]]
