revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP-11|display=title}} {{Location map |Japan Saitama | lat_deg = 36 | lat_min = 05 | lat_sec = 13.8 | lon_deg = 139 | lon_min = 23 | lon_sec = 17.8 | label = Sanzenzuka
Kofun Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 200 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster''' (三千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It was designated a Historic Site by Higashimatsuyama City on February 6, 1956,{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2019-08-22|accessdate=2020-04-04}} and selected as an Important Site by Saitama Prefecture on October 1, 1976.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/documents/02_116_123.pdf|title=Important Sites Selected by Saitama Prefecture|publisher=Saitama Prefecture|date=2020-03-02|accessdate=2020-04-04}} == Overview == Centered around the summit of {{ill|Raidenyama Kofun (Higashimatsuyama)|ja|雷電山古墳 (東松山市)}}, the Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster consists of approximately 250 tumuli distributed across nine sub-clusters on ridges divided by valleys extending radially from the summit. The landscape was significantly altered by the development of a golf course, leading to the destruction of many tumuli. The Benten-zuka Kofun in the third sub-cluster was excavated in 1960, followed by the Akiba-zuka Kofun in the fifth sub-cluster and the Nagatsuka Kofun in the eighth sub-cluster in the following year.{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == Major Kofun == * '''Raidenyama Kofun''' : Considered the main tumulus of the cluster, it is an {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|en|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a total length of 85 meters. The oldest {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} in Saitama Prefecture was unearthed from this mound. * '''Benten-zuka Kofun''' : Numbered as the second mound of the third sub-cluster and considered its main tumulus. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} with a total length of 40 meters, oriented northwest. The burial chamber is presumed to be a vertical stone chamber. Artifacts such as {{ill|haji ware|en|土師器}}, cylindrical haniwa, and human-shaped haniwa were found, and it is thought to have been built in the late 6th century. * '''Akibayama Kofun''' : Also called Akiba-zuka. It is the main tumulus of the fifth sub-cluster and is closest to Raidenyama Kofun. The tumulus is a 44.5-meter-long keyhole-shaped mound, with a 7-meter-long lateral stone chamber in the rear circular section and a 1.7-meter-long vertical stone chamber in the front section. Two tsuba (sword guards) were excavated from the lateral stone chamber. The absence of haniwa suggests it was built in the late 6th century. * '''Nagatsuka Kofun''' : A keyhole-shaped tumulus utilizing natural topography, numbered as the first mound of the eighth sub-cluster. The tumulus is 35 meters in length, with a rear circular section diameter of 24 meters and a front section length and width of 11 meters. The rear section contains a 5.7-meter-long right-sided lateral stone chamber, and the front section presumably contains a 2.3-meter-long vertical stone chamber. Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}} File:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated horse-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|last=Shiono|first=Hiroshi|title=Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|year=2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == See Also == *{{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} *{{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} == External Links == *[http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City {{Archaeology}} [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Higashimatsuyama History]] [[Category:Kofun Clusters]] [[Category:Adding a category]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sanchinzuka Kofun Group-qq]] [[Category:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:01-17]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] {{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP-11|display=title}} {{Location map |Japan Saitama | lat_deg = 36 | lat_min = 05 | lat_sec = 13.8 | lon_deg = 139 | lon_min = 23 | lon_sec = 17.8 | label = Sanzenzuka

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP-11|display=title}} {{Location map |Japan Saitama | lat_deg = 36 | lat_min = 05 | lat_sec = 13.8 | lon_deg = 139 | lon_min = 23 | lon_sec = 17.8 | label = Sanzenzuka
Kofun Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 200 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster''' (三千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It was designated a Historic Site by Higashimatsuyama City on February 6, 1956,{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2019-08-22|accessdate=2020-04-04}} and selected as an Important Site by Saitama Prefecture on October 1, 1976.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/documents/02_116_123.pdf|title=Important Sites Selected by Saitama Prefecture|publisher=Saitama Prefecture|date=2020-03-02|accessdate=2020-04-04}} == Overview == Centered around the summit of {{ill|Raidenyama Kofun (Higashimatsuyama)|ja|雷電山古墳 (東松山市)}}, the Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster consists of approximately 250 tumuli distributed across nine sub-clusters on ridges divided by valleys extending radially from the summit. The landscape was significantly altered by the development of a golf course, leading to the destruction of many tumuli. The Benten-zuka Kofun in the third sub-cluster was excavated in 1960, followed by the Akiba-zuka Kofun in the fifth sub-cluster and the Nagatsuka Kofun in the eighth sub-cluster in the following year.{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == Major Kofun == * '''Raidenyama Kofun''' : Considered the main tumulus of the cluster, it is an {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|en|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a total length of 85 meters. The oldest {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} in Saitama Prefecture was unearthed from this mound. * '''Benten-zuka Kofun''' : Numbered as the second mound of the third sub-cluster and considered its main tumulus. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} with a total length of 40 meters, oriented northwest. The burial chamber is presumed to be a vertical stone chamber. Artifacts such as {{ill|haji ware|en|土師器}}, cylindrical haniwa, and human-shaped haniwa were found, and it is thought to have been built in the late 6th century. * '''Akibayama Kofun''' : Also called Akiba-zuka. It is the main tumulus of the fifth sub-cluster and is closest to Raidenyama Kofun. The tumulus is a 44.5-meter-long keyhole-shaped mound, with a 7-meter-long lateral stone chamber in the rear circular section and a 1.7-meter-long vertical stone chamber in the front section. Two tsuba (sword guards) were excavated from the lateral stone chamber. The absence of haniwa suggests it was built in the late 6th century. * '''Nagatsuka Kofun''' : A keyhole-shaped tumulus utilizing natural topography, numbered as the first mound of the eighth sub-cluster. The tumulus is 35 meters in length, with a rear circular section diameter of 24 meters and a front section length and width of 11 meters. The rear section contains a 5.7-meter-long right-sided lateral stone chamber, and the front section presumably contains a 2.3-meter-long vertical stone chamber. Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}} File:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated horse-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|last=Shiono|first=Hiroshi|title=Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|year=2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == See Also == *{{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} *{{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} == External Links == *[http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City {{Archaeology}} [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Higashimatsuyama History]] [[Category:Kofun Clusters]] [[Category:Adding a category]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sanchinzuka Kofun Group-qq]] [[Category:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:01-17]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP-11|display=title}} {{Location map |Japan Saitama | lat_deg = 36 | lat_min = 05 | lat_sec = 13.8 | lon_deg = 139 | lon_min = 23 | lon_sec = 17.8 | label = Sanzenzuka
Kofun Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 200 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster''' (三千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It was designated a Historic Site by Higashimatsuyama City on February 6, 1956,{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2019-08-22|accessdate=2020-04-04}} and selected as an Important Site by Saitama Prefecture on October 1, 1976.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/documents/02_116_123.pdf|title=Important Sites Selected by Saitama Prefecture|publisher=Saitama Prefecture|date=2020-03-02|accessdate=2020-04-04}} == Overview == Centered around the summit of {{ill|Raidenyama Kofun (Higashimatsuyama)|ja|雷電山古墳 (東松山市)}}, the Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster consists of approximately 250 tumuli distributed across nine sub-clusters on ridges divided by valleys extending radially from the summit. The landscape was significantly altered by the development of a golf course, leading to the destruction of many tumuli. The Benten-zuka Kofun in the third sub-cluster was excavated in 1960, followed by the Akiba-zuka Kofun in the fifth sub-cluster and the Nagatsuka Kofun in the eighth sub-cluster in the following year.{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == Major Kofun == * '''Raidenyama Kofun''' : Considered the main tumulus of the cluster, it is an {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|en|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a total length of 85 meters. The oldest {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} in Saitama Prefecture was unearthed from this mound. * '''Benten-zuka Kofun''' : Numbered as the second mound of the third sub-cluster and considered its main tumulus. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} with a total length of 40 meters, oriented northwest. The burial chamber is presumed to be a vertical stone chamber. Artifacts such as {{ill|haji ware|en|土師器}}, cylindrical haniwa, and human-shaped haniwa were found, and it is thought to have been built in the late 6th century. * '''Akibayama Kofun''' : Also called Akiba-zuka. It is the main tumulus of the fifth sub-cluster and is closest to Raidenyama Kofun. The tumulus is a 44.5-meter-long keyhole-shaped mound, with a 7-meter-long lateral stone chamber in the rear circular section and a 1.7-meter-long vertical stone chamber in the front section. Two tsuba (sword guards) were excavated from the lateral stone chamber. The absence of haniwa suggests it was built in the late 6th century. * '''Nagatsuka Kofun''' : A keyhole-shaped tumulus utilizing natural topography, numbered as the first mound of the eighth sub-cluster. The tumulus is 35 meters in length, with a rear circular section diameter of 24 meters and a front section length and width of 11 meters. The rear section contains a 5.7-meter-long right-sided lateral stone chamber, and the front section presumably contains a 2.3-meter-long vertical stone chamber. Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}} File:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated horse-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|last=Shiono|first=Hiroshi|title=Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|year=2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == See Also == *{{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} *{{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} == External Links == *[http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City {{Archaeology}} [[Category:Kofun in It was designated a Historic Site by Higashimatsuyama City on February 6, 1956,{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2019-08-22|accessdate=2020-04-04}} and selected as an Important Site by Saitama Prefecture on October 1, 1976.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/documents/02_116_123.pdf|title=Important Sites Selected by Saitama Prefecture|publisher=Saitama Prefecture|date=2020-03-02|accessdate=2020-04-04}} == Overview == Centered around the summit of {{ill|Raidenyama Kofun (Higashimatsuyama)|ja|雷電山古墳 (東松山市)}}, the Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster consists of approximately 250 tumuli distributed across nine sub-clusters on ridges divided by valleys extending radially from the summit. The landscape was significantly altered by the development of a golf course, leading to the destruction of many tumuli. The Benten-zuka Kofun in the third sub-cluster was excavated in 1960, followed by the Akiba-zuka Kofun in the fifth sub-cluster and the Nagatsuka Kofun in the eighth sub-cluster in the following year.{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == Major Kofun == * '''Raidenyama Kofun''' : Considered the main tumulus of the cluster, it is an {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|en|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a total length of 85 meters. The oldest {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} in Saitama Prefecture was unearthed from this mound. * '''Benten-zuka Kofun''' : Numbered as the second mound of the third sub-cluster and considered its main tumulus. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} with a total length of 40 meters, oriented northwest. The burial chamber is presumed to be a vertical stone chamber. Artifacts such as {{ill|haji ware|en|土師器}}, cylindrical haniwa, and human-shaped haniwa were found, and it is thought to have been built in the late 6th century. * '''Akibayama Kofun''' : Also called Akiba-zuka. It is the main tumulus of the fifth sub-cluster and is closest to Raidenyama Kofun. The tumulus is a 44.5-meter-long keyhole-shaped mound, with a 7-meter-long lateral stone chamber in the rear circular section and a 1.7-meter-long vertical stone chamber in the front section. Two tsuba (sword guards) were excavated from the lateral stone chamber. The absence of haniwa suggests it was built in the late 6th century. * '''Nagatsuka Kofun''' : A keyhole-shaped tumulus utilizing natural topography, numbered as the first mound of the eighth sub-cluster. The tumulus is 35 meters in length, with a rear circular section diameter of 24 meters and a front section length and width of 11 meters. The rear section contains a 5.7-meter-long right-sided lateral stone chamber, and the front section presumably contains a 2.3-meter-long vertical stone chamber. Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}} File:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated horse-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|last=Shiono|first=Hiroshi|title=Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|year=2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP-11|display=title}} {{Location map |Japan Saitama | lat_deg = 36 | lat_min = 05 | lat_sec = 13.8 | lon_deg = 139 | lon_min = 23 | lon_sec = 17.8 | label = Sanzenzuka
Kofun Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 200 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster''' (三千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It was designated a Historic Site by Higashimatsuyama City on February 6, 1956,{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2019-08-22|accessdate=2020-04-04}} and selected as an Important Site by Saitama Prefecture on October 1, 1976.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/documents/02_116_123.pdf|title=Important Sites Selected by Saitama Prefecture|publisher=Saitama Prefecture|date=2020-03-02|accessdate=2020-04-04}} == Overview == Centered around the summit of {{ill|Raidenyama Kofun (Higashimatsuyama)|ja|雷電山古墳 (東松山市)}}, the Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster consists of approximately 250 tumuli distributed across nine sub-clusters on ridges divided by valleys extending radially from the summit. The landscape was significantly altered by the development of a golf course, leading to the destruction of many tumuli. The Benten-zuka Kofun in the third sub-cluster was excavated in 1960, followed by the Akiba-zuka Kofun in the fifth sub-cluster and the Nagatsuka Kofun in the eighth sub-cluster in the following year.{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == Major Kofun == * '''Raidenyama Kofun''' : Considered the main tumulus of the cluster, it is an {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|en|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a total length of 85 meters. The oldest {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} in Saitama Prefecture was unearthed from this mound. * '''Benten-zuka Kofun''' : Numbered as the second mound of the third sub-cluster and considered its main tumulus. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳}} with a total length of 40 meters, oriented northwest. The burial chamber is presumed to be a vertical stone chamber. Artifacts such as {{ill|haji ware|en|土師器}}, cylindrical haniwa, and human-shaped haniwa were found, and it is thought to have been built in the late 6th century. * '''Akibayama Kofun''' : Also called Akiba-zuka. It is the main tumulus of the fifth sub-cluster and is closest to Raidenyama Kofun. The tumulus is a 44.5-meter-long keyhole-shaped mound, with a 7-meter-long lateral stone chamber in the rear circular section and a 1.7-meter-long vertical stone chamber in the front section. Two tsuba (sword guards) were excavated from the lateral stone chamber. The absence of haniwa suggests it was built in the late 6th century. * '''Nagatsuka Kofun''' : A keyhole-shaped tumulus utilizing natural topography, numbered as the first mound of the eighth sub-cluster. The tumulus is 35 meters in length, with a rear circular section diameter of 24 meters and a front section length and width of 11 meters. The rear section contains a 5.7-meter-long right-sided lateral stone chamber, and the front section presumably contains a 2.3-meter-long vertical stone chamber. Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}} File:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated horse-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|last=Shiono|first=Hiroshi|title=Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|year=2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == See Also == *{{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} *{{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} == External Links == *[http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City {{Archaeology}} [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Higashimatsuyama History]] [[Category:Kofun Clusters]] [[Category:Adding a category]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sanchinzuka Kofun Group-qq]] [[Category:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:01-17]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] The tumulus is 35 meters in length, with a rear circular section diameter of 24 meters and a front section length and width of 11 meters. The rear section contains a 5.7-meter-long right-sided lateral stone chamber, and the front section presumably contains a 2.3-meter-long vertical stone chamber. Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}} File:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated horse-shaped haniwa

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP-11|display=title}} {{Location map |Japan Saitama | lat_deg = 36 | lat_min = 05 | lat_sec = 13.8 | lon_deg = 139 | lon_min = 23 | lon_sec = 17.8 | label = Sanzenzuka
Kofun Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 200 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster''' (三千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It was designated a Historic Site by Higashimatsuyama City on February 6, 1956,{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2019-08-22|accessdate=2020-04-04}} and selected as an Important Site by Saitama Prefecture on October 1, 1976.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/documents/02_116_123.pdf|title=Important Sites Selected by Saitama Prefecture|publisher=Saitama Prefecture|date=2020-03-02|accessdate=2020-04-04}} == Overview == Centered around the summit of {{ill|Raidenyama Kofun (Higashimatsuyama)|ja|雷電山古墳 (東松山市)}}, the Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster consists of approximately 250 tumuli distributed across nine sub-clusters on ridges divided by valleys extending radially from the summit. The landscape was significantly altered by the development of a golf course, leading to the destruction of many tumuli. The Benten-zuka Kofun in the third sub-cluster was excavated in 1960, followed by the Akiba-zuka Kofun in the fifth sub-cluster and the Nagatsuka Kofun in the eighth sub-cluster in the following year.{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == Major Kofun == * '''Raidenyama Kofun''' : Considered the main tumulus of the cluster, it is an {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|en|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a total length of 85 meters. The oldest {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} in Saitama Prefecture was unearthed from this mound. * '''Benten-zuka Kofun''' : Numbered as the second mound of the third sub-cluster and considered its main tumulus. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} with a total length of 40 meters, oriented northwest. The burial chamber is presumed to be a vertical stone chamber. Artifacts such as {{ill|haji ware|en|土師器}}, cylindrical haniwa, and human-shaped haniwa were found, and it is thought to have been built in the late 6th century. * '''Akibayama Kofun''' : Also called Akiba-zuka. It is the main tumulus of the fifth sub-cluster and is closest to Raidenyama Kofun. The tumulus is a 44.5-meter-long keyhole-shaped mound, with a 7-meter-long lateral stone chamber in the rear circular section and a 1.7-meter-long vertical stone chamber in the front section. Two tsuba (sword guards) were excavated from the lateral stone chamber. The absence of haniwa suggests it was built in the late 6th century. * '''Nagatsuka Kofun''' : A keyhole-shaped tumulus utilizing natural topography, numbered as the first mound of the eighth sub-cluster. The tumulus is 35 meters in length, with a rear circular section diameter of 24 meters and a front section length and width of 11 meters. The rear section contains a 5.7-meter-long right-sided lateral stone chamber, and the front section presumably contains a 2.3-meter-long vertical stone chamber. Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}} File:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated horse-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|last=Shiono|first=Hiroshi|title=Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|year=2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == See Also == *{{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} *{{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} == External Links == {{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP-11|display=title}} {{Location map |Japan Saitama | lat_deg = 36 | lat_min = 05 | lat_sec = 13.8 | lon_deg = 139 | lon_min = 23 | lon_sec = 17.8 | label = Sanzenzuka
Kofun Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 200 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster''' (三千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It was designated a Historic Site by Higashimatsuyama City on February 6, 1956,{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2019-08-22|accessdate=2020-04-04}} and selected as an Important Site by Saitama Prefecture on October 1, 1976.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/documents/02_116_123.pdf|title=Important Sites Selected by Saitama Prefecture|publisher=Saitama Prefecture|date=2020-03-02|accessdate=2020-04-04}} == Overview == Centered around the summit of {{ill|Raidenyama Kofun (Higashimatsuyama)|ja|雷電山古墳 (東松山市)}}, the Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster consists of approximately 250 tumuli distributed across nine sub-clusters on ridges divided by valleys extending radially from the summit. The landscape was significantly altered by the development of a golf course, leading to the destruction of many tumuli. The Benten-zuka Kofun in the third sub-cluster was excavated in 1960, followed by the Akiba-zuka Kofun in the fifth sub-cluster and the Nagatsuka Kofun in the eighth sub-cluster in the following year.{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == Major Kofun == * '''Raidenyama Kofun''' : Considered the main tumulus of the cluster, it is an {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|en|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a total length of 85 meters. The oldest {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} in Saitama Prefecture was unearthed from this mound. * '''Benten-zuka Kofun''' : Numbered as the second mound of the third sub-cluster and considered its main tumulus. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} with a total length of 40 meters, oriented northwest. The burial chamber is presumed to be a vertical stone chamber. Artifacts such as {{ill|haji ware|en|土師器}}, cylindrical haniwa, and human-shaped haniwa were found, and it is thought to have been built in the late 6th century. * '''Akibayama Kofun''' : Also called Akiba-zuka. It is the main tumulus of the fifth sub-cluster and is closest to Raidenyama Kofun. The tumulus is a 44.5-meter-long keyhole-shaped mound, with a 7-meter-long lateral stone chamber in the rear circular section and a 1.7-meter-long vertical stone chamber in the front section. Two tsuba (sword guards) were excavated from the lateral stone chamber. The absence of haniwa suggests it was built in the late 6th century. * '''Nagatsuka Kofun''' : A keyhole-shaped tumulus utilizing natural topography, numbered as the first mound of the eighth sub-cluster. The tumulus is 35 meters in length, with a rear circular section diameter of 24 meters and a front section length and width of 11 meters. The rear section contains a 5.7-meter-long right-sided lateral stone chamber, and the front section presumably contains a 2.3-meter-long vertical stone chamber. Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}} File:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated horse-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|last=Shiono|first=Hiroshi|title=Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|year=2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == See Also == *{{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} *{{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} == External Links == revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Qq deprecated start]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP-11|display=title}} {{Location map |Japan Saitama | lat_deg = 36 | lat_min = 05 | lat_sec = 13.8 | lon_deg = 139 | lon_min = 23 | lon_sec = 17.8 | label = Sanzenzuka
Kofun Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 200 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster''' (三千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It was designated a Historic Site by Higashimatsuyama City on February 6, 1956,{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2019-08-22|accessdate=2020-04-04}} and selected as an Important Site by Saitama Prefecture on October 1, 1976.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/documents/02_116_123.pdf|title=Important Sites Selected by Saitama Prefecture|publisher=Saitama Prefecture|date=2020-03-02|accessdate=2020-04-04}} == Overview == Centered around the summit of {{ill|Raidenyama Kofun (Higashimatsuyama)|ja|雷電山古墳 (東松山市)}}, the Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster consists of approximately 250 tumuli distributed across nine sub-clusters on ridges divided by valleys extending radially from the summit. The landscape was significantly altered by the development of a golf course, leading to the destruction of many tumuli. The Benten-zuka Kofun in the third sub-cluster was excavated in 1960, followed by the Akiba-zuka Kofun in the fifth sub-cluster and the Nagatsuka Kofun in the eighth sub-cluster in the following year.{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == Major Kofun == * '''Raidenyama Kofun''' : Considered the main tumulus of the cluster, it is an {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|en|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a total length of 85 meters. The oldest {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} in Saitama Prefecture was unearthed from this mound. * '''Benten-zuka Kofun''' : Numbered as the second mound of the third sub-cluster and considered its main tumulus. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} with a total length of 40 meters, oriented northwest. The burial chamber is presumed to be a vertical stone chamber. Artifacts such as {{ill|haji ware|en|土師器}}, cylindrical haniwa, and human-shaped haniwa were found, and it is thought to have been built in the late 6th century. * '''Akibayama Kofun''' : Also called Akiba-zuka. It is the main tumulus of the fifth sub-cluster and is closest to Raidenyama Kofun. The tumulus is a 44.5-meter-long keyhole-shaped mound, with a 7-meter-long lateral stone chamber in the rear circular section and a 1.7-meter-long vertical stone chamber in the front section. Two tsuba (sword guards) were excavated from the lateral stone chamber. The absence of haniwa suggests it was built in the late 6th century. * '''Nagatsuka Kofun''' : A keyhole-shaped tumulus utilizing natural topography, numbered as the first mound of the eighth sub-cluster. The tumulus is 35 meters in length, with a rear circular section diameter of 24 meters and a front section length and width of 11 meters. The rear section contains a 5.7-meter-long right-sided lateral stone chamber, and the front section presumably contains a 2.3-meter-long vertical stone chamber. Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}} File:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated horse-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|last=Shiono|first=Hiroshi|title=Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|year=2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == See Also == *{{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} *{{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} == External Links == *[http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City {{Archaeology}} [[Category:Kofun in revision.text:
{{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP-11|display=title}} {{Location map |Japan Saitama | lat_deg = 36 | lat_min = 05 | lat_sec = 13.8 | lon_deg = 139 | lon_min = 23 | lon_sec = 17.8 | label = Sanzenzuka
Kofun Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 200 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster''' (三千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It was designated a Historic Site by Higashimatsuyama City on February 6, 1956,{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2019-08-22|accessdate=2020-04-04}} and selected as an Important Site by Saitama Prefecture on October 1, 1976.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/documents/02_116_123.pdf|title=Important Sites Selected by Saitama Prefecture|publisher=Saitama Prefecture|date=2020-03-02|accessdate=2020-04-04}} == Overview == Centered around the summit of {{ill|Raidenyama Kofun (Higashimatsuyama)|ja|雷電山古墳 (東松山市)}}, the Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster consists of approximately 250 tumuli distributed across nine sub-clusters on ridges divided by valleys extending radially from the summit. The landscape was significantly altered by the development of a golf course, leading to the destruction of many tumuli. The Benten-zuka Kofun in the third sub-cluster was excavated in 1960, followed by the Akiba-zuka Kofun in the fifth sub-cluster and the Nagatsuka Kofun in the eighth sub-cluster in the following year.{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == Major Kofun == * '''Raidenyama Kofun''' : Considered the main tumulus of the cluster, it is an {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|en|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a total length of 85 meters. The oldest {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} in Saitama Prefecture was unearthed from this mound. * '''Benten-zuka Kofun''' : Numbered as the second mound of the third sub-cluster and considered its main tumulus. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} with a total length of 40 meters, oriented northwest. The burial chamber is presumed to be a vertical stone chamber. Artifacts such as {{ill|haji ware|en|土師器}}, cylindrical haniwa, and human-shaped haniwa were found, and it is thought to have been built in the late 6th century. * '''Akibayama Kofun''' : Also called Akiba-zuka. It is the main tumulus of the fifth sub-cluster and is closest to Raidenyama Kofun. The tumulus is a 44.5-meter-long keyhole-shaped mound, with a 7-meter-long lateral stone chamber in the rear circular section and a 1.7-meter-long vertical stone chamber in the front section. Two tsuba (sword guards) were excavated from the lateral stone chamber. The absence of haniwa suggests it was built in the late 6th century. * '''Nagatsuka Kofun''' : A keyhole-shaped tumulus utilizing natural topography, numbered as the first mound of the eighth sub-cluster. The tumulus is 35 meters in length, with a rear circular section diameter of 24 meters and a front section length and width of 11 meters. The rear section contains a 5.7-meter-long right-sided lateral stone chamber, and the front section presumably contains a 2.3-meter-long vertical stone chamber. Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}} File:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated horse-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|last=Shiono|first=Hiroshi|title=Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|year=2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == See Also == *{{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} *{{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} == External Links == {{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP-11|display=title}} {{Location map |Japan Saitama | lat_deg = 36 | lat_min = 05 | lat_sec = 13.8 | lon_deg = 139 | lon_min = 23 | lon_sec = 17.8 | label = Sanzenzuka
Kofun Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 200 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster''' (三千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It was designated a Historic Site by Higashimatsuyama City on February 6, 1956,{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2019-08-22|accessdate=2020-04-04}} and selected as an Important Site by Saitama Prefecture on October 1, 1976.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/documents/02_116_123.pdf|title=Important Sites Selected by Saitama Prefecture|publisher=Saitama Prefecture|date=2020-03-02|accessdate=2020-04-04}} == Overview == Centered around the summit of {{ill|Raidenyama Kofun (Higashimatsuyama)|ja|雷電山古墳 (東松山市)}}, the Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster consists of approximately 250 tumuli distributed across nine sub-clusters on ridges divided by valleys extending radially from the summit. The landscape was significantly altered by the development of a golf course, leading to the destruction of many tumuli. The Benten-zuka Kofun in the third sub-cluster was excavated in 1960, followed by the Akiba-zuka Kofun in the fifth sub-cluster and the Nagatsuka Kofun in the eighth sub-cluster in the following year.{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == Major Kofun == * '''Raidenyama Kofun''' : Considered the main tumulus of the cluster, it is an {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|en|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a total length of 85 meters. The oldest {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} in Saitama Prefecture was unearthed from this mound. * '''Benten-zuka Kofun''' : Numbered as the second mound of the third sub-cluster and considered its main tumulus. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} with a total length of 40 meters, oriented northwest. The burial chamber is presumed to be a vertical stone chamber. Artifacts such as {{ill|haji ware|en|土師器}}, cylindrical haniwa, and human-shaped haniwa were found, and it is thought to have been built in the late 6th century. * '''Akibayama Kofun''' : Also called Akiba-zuka. It is the main tumulus of the fifth sub-cluster and is closest to Raidenyama Kofun. The tumulus is a 44.5-meter-long keyhole-shaped mound, with a 7-meter-long lateral stone chamber in the rear circular section and a 1.7-meter-long vertical stone chamber in the front section. Two tsuba (sword guards) were excavated from the lateral stone chamber. The absence of haniwa suggests it was built in the late 6th century. * '''Nagatsuka Kofun''' : A keyhole-shaped tumulus utilizing natural topography, numbered as the first mound of the eighth sub-cluster. The tumulus is 35 meters in length, with a rear circular section diameter of 24 meters and a front section length and width of 11 meters. The rear section contains a 5.7-meter-long right-sided lateral stone chamber, and the front section presumably contains a 2.3-meter-long vertical stone chamber. Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}} File:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated horse-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|last=Shiono|first=Hiroshi|title=Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|year=2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == See Also == *{{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} *{{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} == External Links == *[http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City {{Archaeology}} [[Category:Kofun in {{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP-11|display=title}} {{Location map |Japan Saitama | lat_deg = 36 | lat_min = 05 | lat_sec = 13.8 | lon_deg = 139 | lon_min = 23 | lon_sec = 17.8 | label = Sanzenzuka
Kofun Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 200 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster''' (三千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It was designated a Historic Site by Higashimatsuyama City on February 6, 1956,{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2019-08-22|accessdate=2020-04-04}} and selected as an Important Site by Saitama Prefecture on October 1, 1976.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/documents/02_116_123.pdf|title=Important Sites Selected by Saitama Prefecture|publisher=Saitama Prefecture|date=2020-03-02|accessdate=2020-04-04}} == Overview == Centered around the summit of {{ill|Raidenyama Kofun (Higashimatsuyama)|ja|雷電山古墳 (東松山市)}}, the Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster consists of approximately 250 tumuli distributed across nine sub-clusters on ridges divided by valleys extending radially from the summit. The landscape was significantly altered by the development of a golf course, leading to the destruction of many tumuli. The Benten-zuka Kofun in the third sub-cluster was excavated in 1960, followed by the Akiba-zuka Kofun in the fifth sub-cluster and the Nagatsuka Kofun in the eighth sub-cluster in the following year.{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == Major Kofun == * '''Raidenyama Kofun''' : Considered the main tumulus of the cluster, it is an {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|en|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a total length of 85 meters. The oldest {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} in Saitama Prefecture was unearthed from this mound. * '''Benten-zuka Kofun''' : Numbered as the second mound of the third sub-cluster and considered its main tumulus. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} with a total length of 40 meters, oriented northwest. The burial chamber is presumed to be a vertical stone chamber. Artifacts such as {{ill|haji ware|en|土師器}}, cylindrical haniwa, and human-shaped haniwa were found, and it is thought to have been built in the late 6th century. * '''Akibayama Kofun''' : Also called Akiba-zuka. It is the main tumulus of the fifth sub-cluster and is closest to Raidenyama Kofun. The tumulus is a 44.5-meter-long keyhole-shaped mound, with a 7-meter-long lateral stone chamber in the rear circular section and a 1.7-meter-long vertical stone chamber in the front section. Two tsuba (sword guards) were excavated from the lateral stone chamber. The absence of haniwa suggests it was built in the late 6th century. * '''Nagatsuka Kofun''' : A keyhole-shaped tumulus utilizing natural topography, numbered as the first mound of the eighth sub-cluster. The tumulus is 35 meters in length, with a rear circular section diameter of 24 meters and a front section length and width of 11 meters. The rear section contains a 5.7-meter-long right-sided lateral stone chamber, and the front section presumably contains a 2.3-meter-long vertical stone chamber. Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}} File:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated horse-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|last=Shiono|first=Hiroshi|title=Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|year=2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == See Also == *{{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} *{{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} == External Links == *[http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City {{Archaeology}} [[Category:Kofun in revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP-11|display=title}} {{Location map |Japan Saitama | lat_deg = 36 | lat_min = 05 | lat_sec = 13.8 | lon_deg = 139 | lon_min = 23 | lon_sec = 17.8 | label = Sanzenzuka
Kofun Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 200 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster''' (三千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It was designated a Historic Site by Higashimatsuyama City on February 6, 1956,{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2019-08-22|accessdate=2020-04-04}} and selected as an Important Site by Saitama Prefecture on October 1, 1976.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/documents/02_116_123.pdf|title=Important Sites Selected by Saitama Prefecture|publisher=Saitama Prefecture|date=2020-03-02|accessdate=2020-04-04}} == Overview == Centered around the summit of {{ill|Raidenyama Kofun (Higashimatsuyama)|ja|雷電山古墳 (東松山市)}}, the Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster consists of approximately 250 tumuli distributed across nine sub-clusters on ridges divided by valleys extending radially from the summit. The landscape was significantly altered by the development of a golf course, leading to the destruction of many tumuli. The Benten-zuka Kofun in the third sub-cluster was excavated in 1960, followed by the Akiba-zuka Kofun in the fifth sub-cluster and the Nagatsuka Kofun in the eighth sub-cluster in the following year.{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == Major Kofun == * '''Raidenyama Kofun''' : Considered the main tumulus of the cluster, it is an {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|en|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a total length of 85 meters. The oldest {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} in Saitama Prefecture was unearthed from this mound. * '''Benten-zuka Kofun''' : Numbered as the second mound of the third sub-cluster and considered its main tumulus. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} with a total length of 40 meters, oriented northwest. The burial chamber is presumed to be a vertical stone chamber. Artifacts such as {{ill|haji ware|en|土師器}}, cylindrical haniwa, and human-shaped haniwa were found, and it is thought to have been built in the late 6th century. * '''Akibayama Kofun''' : Also called Akiba-zuka. It is the main tumulus of the fifth sub-cluster and is closest to Raidenyama Kofun. The tumulus is a 44.5-meter-long keyhole-shaped mound, with a 7-meter-long lateral stone chamber in the rear circular section and a 1.7-meter-long vertical stone chamber in the front section. Two tsuba (sword guards) were excavated from the lateral stone chamber. The absence of haniwa suggests it was built in the late 6th century. * '''Nagatsuka Kofun''' : A keyhole-shaped tumulus utilizing natural topography, numbered as the first mound of the eighth sub-cluster. The tumulus is 35 meters in length, with a rear circular section diameter of 24 meters and a front section length and width of 11 meters. The rear section contains a 5.7-meter-long right-sided lateral stone chamber, and the front section presumably contains a 2.3-meter-long vertical stone chamber. Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}} File:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated horse-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|last=Shiono|first=Hiroshi|title=Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|year=2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == See Also == *{{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} *{{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} == External Links == *[http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City {{Archaeology}} [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Higashimatsuyama History]] [[Category:Kofun Clusters]] [[Category:Adding a category]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sanchinzuka Kofun revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP-11|display=title}} {{Location map |Japan Saitama | lat_deg = 36 | lat_min = 05 | lat_sec = 13.8 | lon_deg = 139 | lon_min = 23 | lon_sec = 17.8 | label = Sanzenzuka
Kofun Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 200 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster''' (三千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It was designated a Historic Site by Higashimatsuyama City on February 6, 1956,{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2019-08-22|accessdate=2020-04-04}} and selected as an Important Site by Saitama Prefecture on October 1, 1976.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/documents/02_116_123.pdf|title=Important Sites Selected by Saitama Prefecture|publisher=Saitama Prefecture|date=2020-03-02|accessdate=2020-04-04}} == Overview == Centered around the summit of {{ill|Raidenyama Kofun (Higashimatsuyama)|ja|雷電山古墳 (東松山市)}}, the Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster consists of approximately 250 tumuli distributed across nine sub-clusters on ridges divided by valleys extending radially from the summit. The landscape was significantly altered by the development of a golf course, leading to the destruction of many tumuli. The Benten-zuka Kofun in the third sub-cluster was excavated in 1960, followed by the Akiba-zuka Kofun in the fifth sub-cluster and the Nagatsuka Kofun in the eighth sub-cluster in the following year.{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == Major Kofun == * '''Raidenyama Kofun''' : Considered the main tumulus of the cluster, it is an {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|en|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a total length of 85 meters. The oldest {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} in Saitama Prefecture was unearthed from this mound. * '''Benten-zuka Kofun''' : Numbered as the second mound of the third sub-cluster and considered its main tumulus. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} with a total length of 40 meters, oriented northwest. The burial chamber is presumed to be a vertical stone chamber. Artifacts such as {{ill|haji ware|en|土師器}}, cylindrical haniwa, and human-shaped haniwa were found, and it is thought to have been built in the late 6th century. * '''Akibayama Kofun''' : Also called Akiba-zuka. It is the main tumulus of the fifth sub-cluster and is closest to Raidenyama Kofun. The tumulus is a 44.5-meter-long keyhole-shaped mound, with a 7-meter-long lateral stone chamber in the rear circular section and a 1.7-meter-long vertical stone chamber in the front section. Two tsuba (sword guards) were excavated from the lateral stone chamber. The absence of haniwa suggests it was built in the late 6th century. * '''Nagatsuka Kofun''' : A keyhole-shaped tumulus utilizing natural topography, numbered as the first mound of the eighth sub-cluster. The tumulus is 35 meters in length, with a rear circular section diameter of 24 meters and a front section length and width of 11 meters. The rear section contains a 5.7-meter-long right-sided lateral stone chamber, and the front section presumably contains a 2.3-meter-long vertical stone chamber. Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}} File:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated horse-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|last=Shiono|first=Hiroshi|title=Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|year=2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == See Also == *{{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} *{{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} == External Links == *[http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City {{Archaeology}} [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Higashimatsuyama History]] [[Category:Kofun Clusters]] [[Category:Adding a category]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:completion revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:completion" to "[[Category:remove new pages from these]][[Category:Completion"
revision.text:
{{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP-11|display=title}} {{Location map |Japan Saitama | lat_deg = 36 | lat_min = 05 | lat_sec = 13.8 | lon_deg = 139 | lon_min = 23 | lon_sec = 17.8 | label = Sanzenzuka
Kofun Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 200 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster''' (三千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It was designated a Historic Site by Higashimatsuyama City on February 6, 1956,{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2019-08-22|accessdate=2020-04-04}} and selected as an Important Site by Saitama Prefecture on October 1, 1976.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/documents/02_116_123.pdf|title=Important Sites Selected by Saitama Prefecture|publisher=Saitama Prefecture|date=2020-03-02|accessdate=2020-04-04}} == Overview == Centered around the summit of {{ill|Raidenyama Kofun (Higashimatsuyama)|ja|雷電山古墳 (東松山市)}}, the Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster consists of approximately 250 tumuli distributed across nine sub-clusters on ridges divided by valleys extending radially from the summit. The landscape was significantly altered by the development of a golf course, leading to the destruction of many tumuli. The Benten-zuka Kofun in the third sub-cluster was excavated in 1960, followed by the Akiba-zuka Kofun in the fifth sub-cluster and the Nagatsuka Kofun in the eighth sub-cluster in the following year.{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == Major Kofun == * '''Raidenyama Kofun''' : Considered the main tumulus of the cluster, it is an {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|en|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a total length of 85 meters. The oldest {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} in Saitama Prefecture was unearthed from this mound. * '''Benten-zuka Kofun''' : Numbered as the second mound of the third sub-cluster and considered its main tumulus. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} with a total length of 40 meters, oriented northwest. The burial chamber is presumed to be a vertical stone chamber. Artifacts such as {{ill|haji ware|en|土師器}}, cylindrical haniwa, and human-shaped haniwa were found, and it is thought to have been built in the late 6th century. * '''Akibayama Kofun''' : Also called Akiba-zuka. It is the main tumulus of the fifth sub-cluster and is closest to Raidenyama Kofun. The tumulus is a 44.5-meter-long keyhole-shaped mound, with a 7-meter-long lateral stone chamber in the rear circular section and a 1.7-meter-long vertical stone chamber in the front section. Two tsuba (sword guards) were excavated from the lateral stone chamber. The absence of haniwa suggests it was built in the late 6th century. * '''Nagatsuka Kofun''' : A keyhole-shaped tumulus utilizing natural topography, numbered as the first mound of the eighth sub-cluster. The tumulus is 35 meters in length, with a rear circular section diameter of 24 meters and a front section length and width of 11 meters. The rear section contains a 5.7-meter-long right-sided lateral stone chamber, and the front section presumably contains a 2.3-meter-long vertical stone chamber. Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}} File:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated horse-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|last=Shiono|first=Hiroshi|title=Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|year=2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == See Also == *{{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} *{{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} == External Links == *[http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City {{Archaeology}} [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Higashimatsuyama History]] [[Category:Kofun Clusters]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:remove new pages from revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Goodbye $1]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP-11|display=title}} {{Location map |Japan Saitama | lat_deg = 36 | lat_min = 05 | lat_sec = 13.8 | lon_deg = 139 | lon_min = 23 | lon_sec = 17.8 | label = Sanzenzuka
Kofun Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 200 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster''' (三千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It was designated a Historic Site by Higashimatsuyama City on February 6, 1956,{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2019-08-22|accessdate=2020-04-04}} and selected as an Important Site by Saitama Prefecture on October 1, 1976.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/documents/02_116_123.pdf|title=Important Sites Selected by Saitama Prefecture|publisher=Saitama Prefecture|date=2020-03-02|accessdate=2020-04-04}} == Overview == Centered around the summit of {{ill|Raidenyama Kofun (Higashimatsuyama)|ja|雷電山古墳 (東松山市)}}, the Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster consists of approximately 250 tumuli distributed across nine sub-clusters on ridges divided by valleys extending radially from the summit. The landscape was significantly altered by the development of a golf course, leading to the destruction of many tumuli. The Benten-zuka Kofun in the third sub-cluster was excavated in 1960, followed by the Akiba-zuka Kofun in the fifth sub-cluster and the Nagatsuka Kofun in the eighth sub-cluster in the following year.{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == Major Kofun == * '''Raidenyama Kofun''' : Considered the main tumulus of the cluster, it is an {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|en|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a total length of 85 meters. The oldest {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} in Saitama Prefecture was unearthed from this mound. * '''Benten-zuka Kofun''' : Numbered as the second mound of the third sub-cluster and considered its main tumulus. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} with a total length of 40 meters, oriented northwest. The burial chamber is presumed to be a vertical stone chamber. Artifacts such as {{ill|haji ware|en|土師器}}, cylindrical haniwa, and human-shaped haniwa were found, and it is thought to have been built in the late 6th century. * '''Akibayama Kofun''' : Also called Akiba-zuka. It is the main tumulus of the fifth sub-cluster and is closest to Raidenyama Kofun. The tumulus is a 44.5-meter-long keyhole-shaped mound, with a 7-meter-long lateral stone chamber in the rear circular section and a 1.7-meter-long vertical stone chamber in the front section. Two tsuba (sword guards) were excavated from the lateral stone chamber. The absence of haniwa suggests it was built in the late 6th century. * '''Nagatsuka Kofun''' : A keyhole-shaped tumulus utilizing natural topography, numbered as the first mound of the eighth sub-cluster. The tumulus is 35 meters in length, with a rear circular section diameter of 24 meters and a front section length and width of 11 meters. The rear section contains a 5.7-meter-long right-sided lateral stone chamber, and the front section presumably contains a 2.3-meter-long vertical stone chamber. Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}} File:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated horse-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|last=Shiono|first=Hiroshi|title=Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|year=2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == See Also == *{{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} *{{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} == External Links == {{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP-11|display=title}} {{Location map |Japan Saitama | lat_deg = 36 | lat_min = 05 | lat_sec = 13.8 | lon_deg = 139 | lon_min = 23 | lon_sec = 17.8 | label = Sanzenzuka
Kofun Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 200 | caption = {{Center|Location}} }} '''Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster''' (三千塚古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. It was designated a Historic Site by Higashimatsuyama City on February 6, 1956,{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2019-08-22|accessdate=2020-04-04}} and selected as an Important Site by Saitama Prefecture on October 1, 1976.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/documents/02_116_123.pdf|title=Important Sites Selected by Saitama Prefecture|publisher=Saitama Prefecture|date=2020-03-02|accessdate=2020-04-04}} == Overview == Centered around the summit of {{ill|Raidenyama Kofun (Higashimatsuyama)|ja|雷電山古墳 (東松山市)}}, the Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster consists of approximately 250 tumuli distributed across nine sub-clusters on ridges divided by valleys extending radially from the summit. The landscape was significantly altered by the development of a golf course, leading to the destruction of many tumuli. The Benten-zuka Kofun in the third sub-cluster was excavated in 1960, followed by the Akiba-zuka Kofun in the fifth sub-cluster and the Nagatsuka Kofun in the eighth sub-cluster in the following year.{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == Major Kofun == * '''Raidenyama Kofun''' : Considered the main tumulus of the cluster, it is an {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|en|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a total length of 85 meters. The oldest {{ill|haniwa|en|埴輪}} in Saitama Prefecture was unearthed from this mound. * '''Benten-zuka Kofun''' : Numbered as the second mound of the third sub-cluster and considered its main tumulus. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun (keyhole-shaped tumulus)|en|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} with a total length of 40 meters, oriented northwest. The burial chamber is presumed to be a vertical stone chamber. Artifacts such as {{ill|haji ware|en|土師器}}, cylindrical haniwa, and human-shaped haniwa were found, and it is thought to have been built in the late 6th century. * '''Akibayama Kofun''' : Also called Akiba-zuka. It is the main tumulus of the fifth sub-cluster and is closest to Raidenyama Kofun. The tumulus is a 44.5-meter-long keyhole-shaped mound, with a 7-meter-long lateral stone chamber in the rear circular section and a 1.7-meter-long vertical stone chamber in the front section. Two tsuba (sword guards) were excavated from the lateral stone chamber. The absence of haniwa suggests it was built in the late 6th century. * '''Nagatsuka Kofun''' : A keyhole-shaped tumulus utilizing natural topography, numbered as the first mound of the eighth sub-cluster. The tumulus is 35 meters in length, with a rear circular section diameter of 24 meters and a front section length and width of 11 meters. The rear section contains a 5.7-meter-long right-sided lateral stone chamber, and the front section presumably contains a 2.3-meter-long vertical stone chamber. Both were looted, and the only surviving artifact is two silver rings from the lateral stone chamber. The construction date is estimated to be in the late 6th century, slightly earlier than Akiba-zuka. File:三千塚古墳群出土 頭椎大刀.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated chokutō (straight sword)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}} File:三千塚古墳群出土 馬形埴輪.JPG|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster excavated horse-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|last=Shiono|first=Hiroshi|title=Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu|publisher=Sakitama Publishing|year=2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|author=Higashimatsuyama Board of Education|title=Excavation Report No. 29: Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|year=2012|ref=harv}} == See Also == *{{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} *{{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} == External Links == *[http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikubu/bunkazai/menu/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_ichiran.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City {{Archaeology}} [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Higashimatsuyama History]] [[Category:Kofun Clusters]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:remove new pages from these]][[Category:C]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sanchinzuka Kofun Group]][[Category:11]]
